title: Invitation to Amplify (and a comped hotel room in Vegas)
questions: How do I register for the Amplify conference and get the free hotel room?
What is the deadline to qualify for the complimentary hotel and admission?
How does the hotel deposit work and when is it refunded or applied?
What are the cancellation requirements to avoid losing the deposit?
search_terms: hotel deposit refund policy one-night deposit
how to book hotel after registration confirmation
summary: This post invites readers to a hybrid conference in Las Vegas (with streaming access) and explains how to claim complimentary hotel and admission by using a specific promo code before a deadline. It is aimed at potential attendees who need clear, step-by-step guidance for registration, hotel booking, and the deposit/cancellation rules.

title: Invitation to Amplify (as my guest)
questions: What conferences in product analytics and experimentation are happening soon?
How should teams decide between virtual and in-person attendance?
What incentives or registration codes can help drive event participation?
How does UTM tracking factor into event registration links?
summary: The post is an invitation to readers to attend a hybrid conference focused on product analytics and experimentation, with both virtual and in-person options. It targets product practitioners and teams who want to learn from speakers and consider whether to travel or attend remotely. It also mentions using a free registration code and includes a note about UTM tracking for the registration link.

title: TBM 49/53: Signals and Confidence
questions: Which observable signals along the customer journey predict loyalty and high LTV?
How can we make and communicate our assumptions about customer behavior without pretending we know everything?
What should we instrument in the product to test these signals?
How do we move from intuition to evidence using correlation and causation analysis?
summary: This post presents a lightweight workshop activity for teams to map observable customer signals onto key milestones in a customer journey. It’s designed to help product and analytics teams surface uncertain assumptions about what predicts loyalty and high lifetime value, then translate those assumptions into measurable events to instrument and test. The approach is especially useful when teams struggle to discuss uncertainty and want a fast way to identify what to measure.
